metadata.title: When Algorithms Become Wisdom
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The book's scope is ambitious without sprawling, staying disciplined across its chapters. Christian and Griffiths systematically map computational solutions onto everyday human dilemmas. Eleven chapters each take one class of problem from computer science to life. A useful move is offering two concrete options rather than an open-ended search.

artifactLines: The book's scope is ambitious without sprawling, staying disciplined across its chapters.
Christian and Griffiths systematically map computational solutions onto everyday human dilemmas.
Eleven chapters each take one class of problem from computer science to life.
A useful move is offering two concrete options rather than an open-ended search.

beats.narration_text: The claim that Christian and Griffiths map computational solutions onto human dilemmas across eleven chapters is what I want to test on my own life. I'll take one recurring decision I make and apply an algorithm to it. Help me. Give me: (1) one chapter's algorithm restated in plain terms, (2) a step-by-step of applying it to a real everyday choice like when to stop searching, (3) where the analogy breaks down and I should offer two concrete options instead.

props.command: The claim that Christian and Griffiths map computational solutions onto human dilemmas across eleven chapters is what I want to test on my own life. I'll take one recurring decision I make and apply an algorithm to it. Help me. Give me: (1) one chapter's algorithm restated in plain terms, (2) a step-by-step of applying it to a real everyday choice like when to stop searching, (3) where the analogy breaks down and I should offer two concrete options instead.
output: ment viewing in San Francisco, rent-stressed and decision-paralyz

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — build

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'When Algorithms Become Wisdom' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each.… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'When Algorithms Become Wisdom' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
